messages.content:
You assess if the provided [Keyword Cluster] are suitable for the given website. Then you either approve without edits, approve with small edits, or don't approve.

Your assesment falls into 3 options.
1 - "yes" the entire keyword cluster is usable as is and you do not edit anything.
2 - "yes" the keyword cluster is usable but youo add small changes to the keyword list, such as removing a few keyords that are too generic or does not fit the category and site.
3 - "no" the keyword cluster is not usable because it either needs large edits, with too many generic keywords, or the whole category of keywords does not allign with the sites core objective, and therefore the whole thing needs to be redone.

You output either a "yes" plus the keywords cluster, "yes" plus a slightly edited keywords cluster, or a "no" plus the reason why of the answer is "no" and what to change and focus on when redoing it.

It is OK to take option 2 and edit/change most of a category keywords if the majority of the other categories are good and can be used as is. Think 70/30. If the keyword cluster would be "perfect" if you edit/change 30% of the keywords, then take option 2 and make the updates yourself. If too many keywords are missing the mark, then take option 3.

Make an objective assesment.
We do not want to have redundant keywords.
Since each keyword will be converted into a full article, each selected keyword must hold it's own unique search intent. E.g. "startup success" and "startup success factors" should not both be selected. Here the action (option 2 - small modifications) should be to remove one of the keywords preserving the keyword with the most specific search intent. In this case "startup success factors" should be keept.

Example of similar keywords but both have a unique search intent:
"startup growth strategies" and "startup success principles" <-- Here we keep both Keywords as they each hold unique search intent.

Example of bad keywords that are too generic: "technology news" "tech industry trends" "emerging technologies" etc.
These keywords are category keywords without specific search intent. All keywords will be turned into articles, and they keyword must contain a specific search intent to gain knowledge.

When the keyword cluster includes too many generic and search intent duplicates, you should choose option 3 "no" and specify what to change to the Seed Keyword selection and the Keyword List selection.

Your output is always formatted as JSON.
You output the JSON and nothing else.

Output example for yes option 1:
{
  "Is KW Cluster Ok": "yes",
  "What to change with KW Seed words": "Na",
  "What to change with KW List": "Na",
  "Categories": [
    {
      "Category": "success",
      "Keywords": [
        "startup growth strategies",
        "startup leadership and success"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Category": "tech",
      "Keywords": [
        "computer vision technology",
        "wearable tech trends"
      ]
    }
  ]
}


Output example for yes option 2:
{
  "Is KW Cluster Ok": "yes",
  "What to change with KW Seed words": "Na",
  "What to change with KW List": "Write a short description about what you changed in the keyword list",
  "Categories": [
    {
      "Category": "success",
      "Keywords": [
        "startup growth strategies",
        "startup leadership and success"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Category": "tech",
      "Keywords": [
        "computer vision technology",
        "wearable tech trends"
      ]
    }
  ]
}


Output example for no option 3:
{
  "Is KW Cluster Ok": "no",
  "What to change with KW Seed words": "Explain what type of seed keywords should be tried in order to yeild better results",
  "What to change with KW List": "Explain what type of keywords should be selected and why in order to yeild better results",
  "Categories": "Na"
}

Please assess if the provided [Keyword Cluster] is suitable for the website.

Every single keyword will be used to create an article.

Your assesment and output falls into 3 categories.
1 - "yes" the entire keyword cluster is usable as is without any modifications.
2 - "yes" the keyword cluster is usable but with small modifications.
3 - "no" the keyword cluster is not suitable and will need to be redone.

Your output is always formatted as JSON.
You output the JSON and nothing else.

[Keyword Cluster]=
{{1.NewKeywords}}

[Website Info]=
{{1.siteInfo}}

"lastKeywordSeedsWordsAttempt":"",

"count":{{(1.count + 1)}},
"ds-1":"{{1.`ds-1`}}",

}
